title: The Mice in Council
lines: The Mice, suffering greatly from the attacks of a Cat, held a great council to decide what to do.
Many plans were suggested, but all were rejected as too dangerous.
At last a young Mouse stood up.
"I have a splendid plan," he announced.
"We will tie a bell to the Cat's neck; then we shall hear her coming and have time to hide."
This plan was received with great approval.
But when the vote was taken as to who would tie on the bell, not a single Mouse would undertake the task.
"It is a fine plan," said the oldest Mouse, "but very hard to carry out."
moral: It is easy to propose, but hard to execute.
